prompt: Wide architectural eye-level photograph of a dated 1980s colonial dining room BEFORE renovation. Plain flat painted ceiling with a sprayed popcorn texture, a single dust-coated brass-and-glass colonial-style chandelier hanging dead center. Mauve-pink wall paint, wallpaper border peeling at the chair rail. A worn beige Berber carpet underfoot. The room feels stuck in 1989 — dated, tired, but lived-in. Shot with a 24mm wide architectural lens, dim overcast natural daylight from a single side window, flat soft shadows, slightly cool color temperature. A red plastic toolbox sits open on the carpet as the only saturated color, signaling work has just begun. High-contrast slightly desaturated palette, NOT HGTV bright, NOT real-estate over-lit. Working-contractor proof-of-work feel. No readable text, no logos.
